1.title: Fleeing to the ends of the earth
1.text: Nineveh lay far to the east, but Jonah sailed for Tarshish — likely a port in the far western Mediterranean (Spain). He ran in the exact opposite direction God sent him, as far as a ship could go.

1.question: What did the Lord send after Jonah fled toward Tarshish (Jonah 1:4)?
options: A great wind and a mighty tempest

1.question: Why were the men exceedingly afraid in Jonah 1:10?
options: They knew Jonah fled from the presence of the Lord
They had run out of water

1.question: What did the Lord prepare to swallow Jonah (Jonah 1:17)?

2.title: A psalm from the belly of the fish
2.text: Jonah's prayer in chapter 2 reads like a psalm of thanksgiving, echoing phrases from the Book of Psalms — a reminder that even at the lowest depths, Jonah reaches for words of worship he already knew.

2.template: The waters compassed me about, even to the {0}

2.question: How did Jonah reach dry land at the end of the chapter (Jonah 2:10)?
options: The fish vomited him out upon the dry land

3.question: What did Jonah cry as he entered Nineveh (Jonah 3:4)?
options: Repent, for the kingdom is at hand
Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown

3.question: What did God do when he saw that Nineveh turned from its evil way (Jonah 3:10)?
options: He repented of the evil he had said he would do

3.title: A king in sackcloth
3.text: In Jonah 3:6 the king of Nineveh rises from his throne, removes his royal robe, puts on sackcloth, and sits in ashes — a monarch publicly humbling himself alongside the least of his people.

4.title: Angry that God was merciful
4.text: The strangest turn in the book: Jonah is furious not because his preaching failed, but because it succeeded. He confesses he fled in the first place precisely because he knew God was gracious and would spare Nineveh.

pairs.left: The plant God prepared for shade
What God sent to smite the gourd
The city God chose to spare

4.question: How did the Lord respond to Jonah's anger in Jonah 4:4?
options: Doest thou well to be angry?

4.question: What reason does the Lord give for sparing Nineveh (Jonah 4:11)?
options: More than sixscore thousand persons who cannot discern right hand from left, and much cattle
